output:
# WEAVE: Walk-Restricted Shapley Interaction Attribution for Graph Neural Network Edges

Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning. Graph neural networks (GNNs) now drive high-stakes decisions in molecular discovery, fraud detection, and recommendation, which has made post-hoc explanation indispensable; the scalable state of the art for edge attribution is GNNShap, a KernelSHAP estimator of per-edge Shapley values. We identify a structural limitation we call SHEAR: because every additive Shapley surrogate has the form g(z') = phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i, it is first-order by construction and cannot express the walk-level interaction mass that k-layer message passing actually computes, so it smears joint edge causation into misleading per-edge main effects. We propose WEAVE (Walk-restricted Exact-collapse Attribution of shapley-interaction Values over Edges), which augments the additive surrogate with higher-order interaction terms restricted to edge-tuples that co-occur on a <=L-hop message-passing walk, buying both tractability and faithfulness, and provably collapses to GNNShap at interaction order 1 (E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gated), making it a strict, axiom-preserving, zero-regression extension of a deployed baseline.

## Section 1: Background and Current State of the Art

Graph neural networks reach state-of-the-art results on node classification, link prediction, graph classification, and recommendation by learning representations through message passing [11]: at each layer a node sends messages (MSG), aggregates messages from its neighbors (AGG), and updates its representation (UPD). Concrete instantiations include GCN [12], GAT [14], GraphSAGE [15], GIN [16], and pooling variants such as DiffPool [30], with the message-passing abstraction itself formalized for quantum chemistry [11] and extended to molecular fingerprint learning [13]. For a k-layer GNN the prediction at a node v depends only on v's k-hop computational graph G_c(v). Because a GNN is a complex, non-linear encoding of graph structure and node features, its outputs cannot be read off its inputs, and post-hoc explanation has become essential for trust [20].

The GNN explainability landscape spans several families. Perturbation and mask methods optimize an edge or feature mask to preserve the prediction: GNNExplainer maximizes mutual information between a learned mask and the output [18], while PGExplainer trains a parameterized mask generator for inductive explanation [19], and PGM-Explainer fits an interpretable Bayesian network to local perturbations [10]. Subgraph-search methods select a connected substructure: SubgraphX scores candidate subgraphs with Shapley values via Monte-Carlo tree search [7], and GStarX uses a structure-aware cooperative game over nodes [21]. Counterfactual explainers such as CF-GNNExplainer seek minimal edge edits that flip the prediction [22], and reinforcement-search explainers such as RG-Explainer learn a generation policy for explanatory subgraphs [23]. A distinct relevance-over-walks family, GNN-LRP, decomposes a prediction into contributions along message-passing walks via layer-wise relevance propagation [9]; it is the closest prior art on the question of walk structure.

A separate line imports cooperative game theory. Each edge, node, or feature is a "player," the prediction is the payout, and a player's Shapley value [3] is the weighted average of its marginal contributions across coalitions. KernelSHAP estimates Shapley values efficiently by fitting a weighted linear surrogate [2]. GraphSVX applies Shapley value explanation to GNN nodes and features [8], and GNNShap (Akkas and Azad, KDD 2024) applies KernelSHAP to estimate EDGE Shapley values and engineers it for scale -- GPU batching and efficient coalition sampling -- so that fidelity can be measured at 30% sparsity and over top-10 edges on large graphs such as Reddit and ogbn-products [1][17]. GNNShap is, today, the scalable Shapley-based edge-attribution baseline. Related game-theoretic valuation tools include the Banzhaf power index [27] and Data Shapley for training-data valuation [28], which motivate the efficient-estimation machinery scalable graph explainers inherit.

The defining property of every additive Shapley/KernelSHAP explanation is its surrogate form: g(z') = phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i [2]. This is first-order by construction. It assigns one scalar per player and cannot represent how players combine. The broader explainable-AI community recognized this and introduced interaction indices -- SHAP interaction values [2], the Shapley-Taylor interaction index [4], Faith-Shap [5], and the axiomatic interaction index of Grabisch and Roubens [6] -- that extend Shapley attribution to higher orders while retaining game-theoretic axioms. These interaction methods are not GNN-aware: their natural support is all O(E^2) edge pairs, intractable at the scale GNNShap targets, and they ignore the message-passing structure that dictates which edges can actually interact. The open question this work answers is whether a structure-aware interaction index can be both faithful and scalable while degrading exactly to GNNShap when interactions are absent -- a question existing GNN-attribution evaluation protocols [29] are only beginning to make measurable.

## Section 2: Key Findings and Analysis

Our central observation is a representational-impossibility argument. A k-layer GNN's prediction is a function of length-<=k walks in G_c(v): message passing composes edge contributions multiplicatively and through non-linearities along each walk, so the true marginal value of an edge is conditional on the co-presence of the other edges on its walk [11]. GNNShap, by contrast, assigns each edge a single context-free scalar via an additive surrogate [1]. That surrogate is first-order by construction; the interaction content f(z') - g(z') -- precisely the walk-level synergy -- lies outside its representational range and is projected into per-edge main effects (or absorbed as surrogate residual). This is not an estimation error that more coalition samples fix; it is a structural limit of additivity. We name this limit SHEAR: the additive Shapley surrogate SHEARs walk-level interaction mass off the model's information flow and smears it into per-edge main effects.

SHEAR has three signatures that make it both real and currently invisible. First, it is SILENT under GNNShap's own evaluation: fidelity at 30% sparsity and fidelity over top-k edges measure how the prediction degrades when high-attribution edges are removed, and that degradation is unchanged by how interaction mass was split among those edges -- so the pooled metric cannot detect mis-attribution. Second, SHEAR SCALES with the interaction variance among edges that share walks: the larger the synergy (a conjunctive "AND-gate" motif), the more attribution mass is mis-placed. Third, SHEAR VANISHES exactly in the degenerate regime: a one-layer GNN (no length->=2 walk exists), or a linear/homogeneous readout with no cross-edge multiplicative or non-linear coupling, in which case the prediction is genuinely additive over edges and additive Shapley is exactly correct.

The discriminating example is a planted AND-gate motif. Suppose two edges e1 and e2 are jointly necessary -- the prediction is high only if BOTH are present -- and individually each does little. Additive Shapley provably mis-splits the joint value: by symmetry each edge receives roughly half, so neither main effect reflects the conjunction. A walk-restricted interaction index instead recovers a positive second-order term phi_{e1,e2} that captures the synergy directly, while leaving the main effects near zero. Conversely, on disconnected edges that share no <=L-hop walk, the interaction term is zero BY CONSTRUCTION (structural sparsity, not estimation noise), and in the one-layer or linear-readout regimes every interaction coefficient phi_S is identically zero.

WEAVE introduces walk-restricted Shapley interaction terms: higher-order coefficients drawn from a Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap interaction index [4][5], supported ONLY on edge-tuples that co-occur on a <=L-hop message-passing walk in G_c(v). Walk restriction simultaneously buys tractability (the interaction support is sparse, not O(E^2)) and faithfulness (interactions are admitted only where the model can actually create them). Crucially, at order 1 the admissible-tuple set T_L is empty and WEAVE's surrogate is identically GNNShap's g(z') = phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i, giving a provable, CI-gated exact collapse with E_collapse <= 1e-6. Analytically, this reframes "faithfulness" for GNN explanation from a ranking property (which edges) to a structural property (which edge interactions the model itself computes), and shows that the missing content is not noise but a specific, localizable, recoverable field tied to the model's message-passing walks.

## The Five Core Questions

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**
Can a walk-restricted Shapley interaction index for GNN edges recover the message-passing walk-level interaction mass that GNNShap's additive KernelSHAP surrogate is representationally incapable of expressing, while provably collapsing to GNNShap at interaction order 1?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

(P1) The actual scientific object in high-stakes GNN tasks is JOINT edge causation, and additive attribution structurally cannot express it. GNN explanations increasingly drive consequential decisions: molecular property and drug-discovery pipelines [13][20][24], financial fraud and collusion-ring detection [25], healthcare and recommendation. In all of these, the question that matters scientifically is not "which single edge is important" but "which edges JOINTLY matter" -- the conjunctive / cooperative motif (a functional group of bonds, a closed transaction loop). GNNShap [1], like every additive KernelSHAP-style surrogate, assigns one context-free scalar phi_i per edge under g(z') = phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i [2], so a synergistic pair that only matters together is forced to split its joint value into two misleading main effects. The object practitioners care about is, by construction, unrepresentable in the additive frame. WEAVE restores it via walk-restricted Shapley interaction terms [4][5][6].

(P2) Message passing IS walk composition, so an explainer blind to walk interactions is misaligned with the model class it explains -- a foundational faithfulness gap, not a tweak. A k-layer message-passing GNN [11] -- GCN [12], GAT [14], GraphSAGE [15], GIN [16], and pooling variants such as DiffPool [30] -- computes node representations by composing messages along <=k-hop walks; an edge's marginal effect is conditional on the co-presence of other edges on its walk. An attribution method whose hypothesis class admits only per-edge main effects is therefore mis-specified relative to the function it claims to explain. This is not a minor approximation error: it is a structural mismatch between the explainer's representational capacity and the model's compute. GNN-LRP [9] already recognized that walks are the right relevance unit; WEAVE imports that insight into the game-theoretic Shapley setting where GNNShap [1] and GraphSVX [8] live.

(P3) The loss is SILENT under today's fidelity@sparsity / top-k benchmarks, so the field may be systematically over-trusting additive edge attributions. GNNShap [1] and peers are validated by fidelity@30% sparsity and fidelity@top-k edges -- metrics that score a RANKING of edges and never probe whether interaction mass f(z')-g(z') was correctly attributed versus smeared into main effects. Because the failure is invisible to the dominant evaluation protocol [10][19], a method can look state-of-the-art while being representationally incapable of expressing the thing users read off the explanation. Surfacing this changes how GNN explanations should be validated: it argues for interaction-aware evaluation (co-walk synergy recovery, AND-gate detection) alongside sparsity-fidelity, and warns that current leaderboards may be rewarding confident-but-incomplete attributions.

(P4) Exact collapse makes WEAVE a safe, zero-regression drop-in upgrade to a deployed scalable baseline, maximizing adoption. WEAVE's exact-collapse guarantee -- provable collapse to GNNShap [1] at interaction order 1 / g_int = 0 (admissible tuple set empty, E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gated) -- means a team running GNNShap today can adopt WEAVE with zero behavioral regression in the additive regime and strictly more expressiveness when interactions exist. This is the adoption story that pure interaction indices [4][5][6] lack: they are not extensions of a scalable graph baseline and do not reduce to one. Inheriting GNNShap's GPU-batched sampling on Reddit and ogbn-products [17] keeps WEAVE tractable at scales where generic O(E^2) interaction indices are hopeless. A faithful upgrade that provably contains the incumbent as a special case is the rare combination that survives contact with production.

(P5) A faithful, scalable interaction index opens genuinely new analysis: discovery of AND-gate / cooperative substructures. Once per-edge-tuple walk-restricted Shapley interactions are computable at scale, the explanation becomes a tool for SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERY rather than mere sanity-checking: surfacing functional groups in molecules [13][20][24], collusion rings in fraud graphs [25], and other cooperative motifs that subgraph methods like SubgraphX [7] or GStarX [21] return only as opaque connected blobs, not as a decomposed interaction index. Compared to counterfactual [22] or reinforcement-search [23] explainers, WEAVE yields signed, axiom-grounded [3] coefficients on specific edge tuples, which can be aggregated into motif libraries and compared across models. This turns GNN explanation into a hypothesis-generation instrument for the underlying domain science, with practical payoff wherever the cooperative structure, not the single-edge ranking, is the deliverable.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

Seven independent obstacles stand between the idea and a working method; solving any one does not solve the others.

O1 (THE BINDING OBSTACLE) -- Combinatorial interaction support is intractable, and walk-restriction must be both tractable AND provably the right support. The full higher-order Shapley interaction over edges has O(E^2) pair terms and O(E^k) terms at order k. At Reddit / ogbn-products scale (millions of edges, high-degree nodes), even enumerating order-2 tuples does not run, and the weighted-least-squares design matrix becomes astronomically wide. The naive recipe "just compute SHAP interaction values" is a non-starter. The escape hatch is walk-restriction: only edge-tuples S that co-occur on a common <=L-hop message-passing walk in G_c(v) can carry GNN interaction mass, so the admissible support T_L = {S : |S|>=2, S lies on a common <=L-hop walk} is far smaller than the full power set. But this creates two coupled hard sub-problems. (a) Tractability: enumerating T_L itself can blow up with node degree d and depth L (worst case ~d^L walks); enumeration must be pruned (degree caps, walk deduplication, message-passing reachability) without dropping real synergy. (b) Correctness of the support: we must argue and empirically validate that no interaction mass of a k-layer GNN lives OUTSIDE edge-tuples that share a <=L-hop walk. Choosing L (tied to the layer count k) and showing the restricted support captures the message-passing interaction structure is the crux; variance, collapse, and metrics all depend on O1.

O2 -- Sample complexity and estimator variance for interaction coefficients. Interaction terms add columns to the KernelSHAP design matrix, so we solve a higher-dimensional weighted least-squares than for main effects alone. Each interaction coefficient phi_S needs enough coalition samples that flip the joint membership of all edges in S, which is rarer than flipping a single edge. With a fixed coalition-sampling budget, estimator variance can swamp small but real synergy terms, producing noisy or sign-flipped phi_S. Naive uniform coalition sampling wastes budget on tuples that never co-activate; we need interaction-aware (Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap-consistent) coalition weighting and variance control, or the recovered interactions are unreliable.

O3 -- Which interaction index? Axioms couple the index choice to exact collapse. The Shapley interaction index [6], the Shapley-Taylor interaction index [4], and Faith-Shap [5] give DIFFERENT higher-order decompositions with different axioms (efficiency, symmetry, dummy, and how mass is split between main and interaction terms). We must pick an index whose restriction to T_L (i) preserves an efficiency/completeness property so attributions still sum to the prediction gap, and (ii) reduces EXACTLY to KernelSHAP main effects when the admissible tuple set is empty. Not every index satisfies (ii) cleanly -- the exact-collapse constraint is not a free engineering choice, it constrains the math: index choice and collapse guarantee are coupled.

O4 -- Exact-collapse engineering as a CI gate is numerically fragile. The claim E_collapse = ||attr_WEAVE,order1 - attr_GNNShap|| <= 1e-6, enforced as a CI gate, is harder than it sounds. To hit 1e-6 across seeds and precisions, the two estimators must share RNG / coalition draws (or be made deterministic), the interaction regularization must vanish identically at order 1, and the regression conditioning (any ridge/Tikhonov term, column scaling, solver tolerance) must collapse to GNNShap's exact configuration. Floating point, GPU non-determinism, and differing solve paths can easily push the residual above 1e-6. Making this a gate means it must be reproducible, not just achievable once.

O5 -- Ground-truth scarcity for INTERACTIONS, not just edges. Standard explanation benchmarks (BA-Shapes, Tree-Cycles, MUTAG, and so on) provide ground-truth EDGES (which edges belong to the motif) but NOT ground-truth INTERACTION TERMS (which edge PAIRS are jointly necessary). You cannot measure interaction recovery against a benchmark that has no interaction labels. We must CONSTRUCT synthetic motifs with KNOWN conjunctive structure -- planted AND-gates and XOR-gates, jointly-necessary edge pairs -- where the true synergy phi_{e1,e2} is known by construction, so interaction recovery becomes measurable. Designing motifs whose label truly depends on a conjunction, and where a GNN actually learns that conjunction, is itself non-trivial.

O6 -- Scalability parity with GNNShap. GNNShap's entire value proposition is SCALE (GPU-batched coalition sampling on Reddit / ogbn-products [1][17]). Adding interaction columns increases the design-matrix width and the enumeration cost. If WEAVE drops GPU batching, balloons memory, or runs many times slower, it is not adoptable regardless of fidelity gains. WEAVE must keep GNNShap's batched sampling and stay within a small constant factor (target <=3x) of GNNShap runtime at a fixed sample budget. Hitting interaction faithfulness AND scale simultaneously is in tension.

O7 -- Metric-blindness: existing fidelity cannot reward interaction attribution. fidelity@sparsity and fidelity@top-k score a SET of edges; they are invariant to whether synergy is attributed to a pair phi_{e1,e2} or smeared additively over the two edges. By construction the "silent loss" is invisible to these metrics -- pooled fidelity@sparsity will look ~unchanged. So we need NEW metrics (interaction-recovery score, surrogate reconstruction residual). The danger: a new metric can be gameable (for example, rewarding any non-zero interaction term). We must design metrics that are (a) sensitive to CORRECT interaction attribution and (b) not trivially inflated, and demonstrate non-gameability (interaction terms placed off the true motif are penalized). The binding obstacle is O1 -- it is structural and combinatorial, and everything downstream (O2 statistical, O3 axiomatic, O4 numerical, O5 data, O6 systems, O7 evaluation) presupposes a correct, tractable walk-restricted support.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

We position WEAVE against five near-miss families using a four-criterion wedge. C1 = post-hoc, model-agnostic, with Shapley/game-theoretic axioms (efficiency, symmetry, dummy, linearity). C2 = conditions attribution on message-passing WALK structure (interaction terms supported only on co-walk tuples). C3 = represents higher-order (>=2) interactions. C4 = provable exact collapse to additive GNNShap at order 1 / g_int = 0. Every prior family clears at most 3 of C1-C4, and none clears all four.

(a) Additive Shapley graph explainers -- GNNShap [1], GraphSVX [8]. Clear C1, C4. MISS C2, C3. These satisfy the Shapley axioms (C1) and trivially "collapse" to the additive baseline because they ARE additive (C4 in the degenerate sense -- they are the order-1 point). But they assign one scalar per edge/node and contain no higher-order terms (MISS C3), and they do not condition attribution on co-walk structure beyond using the computation subgraph as support (MISS C2).

(b) Subgraph-search Shapley -- SubgraphX [7], and structure-aware GStarX [21]. Clear C1. MISS C2, C3, C4. SubgraphX scores connected subgraphs with Shapley values via Monte-Carlo tree search (C1). But its output is a single chosen subgraph, not a per-edge interaction-index decomposition (MISS C3); the coalition structure is connectivity-based, not walk-restricted to <=L-hop message-passing tuples (MISS C2); and it provides no exact collapse to the additive GNNShap baseline (MISS C4). GStarX [21] shares these gaps. They tell you WHICH subgraph, never HOW edge tuples interact as a decomposed, collapsible index.

(c) Generic Shapley interaction indices -- Shapley-Taylor [4], Faith-Shap [5], the Shapley interaction index [6], and SHAP interaction values [2]. Clear C1, C3, C4. MISS C2. These are exactly the higher-order, axiom-grounded interaction machinery WEAVE builds on: they satisfy the axioms (C1), represent >=2-order interactions (C3), and reduce to additive Shapley at order 1 (C4). What they lack is any GNN-structure awareness: their natural support is ALL O(E^2) edge pairs (or larger tuples), which is both intractable at Reddit / ogbn-products [17] scale and model-unfaithful, since most pairs never co-occur on a message-passing walk (MISS C2). Nobody had restricted these indices to the co-walk tuple set dictated by message passing [11].

(d) Mask / perturbation / mutual-information explainers -- GNNExplainer [18], PGExplainer [19], PGM-Explainer [10]. MISS C1, C3, C4. These optimize edge/feature masks or fit interpretable surrogates (mutual information, probabilistic graphical models). They carry no Shapley/game-theoretic axioms (MISS C1), produce importance masks rather than higher-order interaction terms (MISS C3), and have no exact-collapse relationship to GNNShap (MISS C4). At most they touch model structure heuristically; they sit entirely outside the game-theoretic frame WEAVE inhabits. Integrated Gradients [26], as a gradient attribution, likewise misses C1's cooperative-game axioms and C3.

(e) Walk-relevance propagation -- GNN-LRP [9]. The CLOSEST on C2. Clear C2, C3-ish. MISS C1, C4. GNN-LRP is the nearest prior art: it decomposes prediction relevance over message-passing WALKS (C2) and yields higher-order, walk-level terms (C3-ish). But it is layer-wise relevance propagation, not a cooperative-game construction: it carries no efficiency/symmetry/dummy/linearity axioms and no Mobius-Shapley interaction coefficient (MISS C1), and it has no exact collapse to the additive Shapley baseline GNNShap (MISS C4). GNN-LRP proves walks are the right unit; it does not make them game-theoretic or collapsible.

The C1-C4 wedge in compact form:

| Family | C1 axioms | C2 walk-restricted | C3 higher-order | C4 exact collapse |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GNNShap [1] / GraphSVX [8] | yes | no | no | yes (degenerate) |
| SubgraphX [7] / GStarX [21] | yes | no | no | no |
| Shapley-Taylor [4] / Faith-Shap [5] / SHAP-int [2][6] | yes | no | yes | yes |
| GNNExplainer [18] / PGExplainer [19] / PGM [10] | no | no | no | no |
| GNN-LRP [9] | no | yes | yes | no |
| WEAVE (this work) | yes | yes | yes | yes |

WEAVE is the SOLE occupant of C1 AND C2 AND C3 AND C4. It is post-hoc and axiom-grounded like the Shapley family [1][3][8] (C1); it restricts interaction support to co-walk edge tuples dictated by k-layer message passing [11], like GNN-LRP [9] but in the game-theoretic frame (C2); it represents >=2-order interactions via Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap terms [4][5][6] (C3); and it provably collapses to GNNShap [1] at order 1 / g_int = 0 (C4). Why it wasn't solved before: three reasons converge. (i) The generic interaction indices [4][5][6] were built for tabular / feature-vector models; their O(E^2)-or-worse support made them intractable and unfaithful on graphs, and nobody tied them to message-passing walk structure. (ii) The GNN-aware walk method [9] came from the relevance-propagation tradition, not cooperative game theory, so it never acquired the Shapley axioms or a collapse guarantee. (iii) The scalable Shapley graph baselines [1][8] were deliberately additive for tractability, and no one had shown that a walk-restricted higher-order Shapley extension can stay sparse, tractable, AND provably contain the baseline as an exact special case. WEAVE is the first to fuse walk-restriction, Shapley-Taylor/Faith-Shap interactions, and exact collapse onto a scalable baseline, which is why the C1-C2-C3-C4 cell was empty until now.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

Method pipeline (WEAVE). (1) Build the computational graph G_c(v): the L-hop message-passing subgraph around the target node/edge/graph v for a k-layer GNN, with L tied to k. (2) Enumerate admissible interaction tuples T_L = { S subset E(G_c(v)) : |S| >= 2, all edges of S lie on a common <=L-hop walk }, via sparse walk enumeration from v outward, bounded by node degree and L, with pruning (degree caps, walk deduplication, message-passing reachability test, and a budget cap on |T_L| keeping highest-reach tuples). Start at order 2; allow order 3 as an ablation. (3) Extend KernelSHAP: build a weighted least-squares whose design matrix Z has one column per edge (main effects phi_i) and one column per tuple S in T_L (interaction terms phi_S), solved under a Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap-consistent kernel weighting [4][5] so the recovered coefficients form a valid restricted interaction index. Use a SPARSE solver -- the matrix is wide but sparse because T_L is walk-local. (4) Knobs: g_int (interaction gate; g_int = 0 -> pure additive GNNShap) and L (interaction order / walk depth; L = 1 -> additive, T_L empty). (5) Exact-collapse verifier: assert E_collapse = || attr_WEAVE,order1 - attr_GNNShap || <= 1e-6 by sharing coalition RNG, zeroing interaction regularization at order 1, and matching solver conditioning, wired as a CI gate that fails the build if exceeded. (6) Keep GNNShap's GPU-batched coalition sampling throughout. Exact collapse: at order 1 / g_int = 0 the admissible tuple set is empty, the interaction columns vanish, and the WLS reduces identically to GNNShap's KernelSHAP solve, so g_WEAVE == g_GNNShap.

Datasets (real names). Synthetic ground-truth-motif benchmarks (known motif edges): BA-Shapes, BA-Community, Tree-Cycles, Tree-Grid; ADD conjunctive variants -- planted AND-gate and XOR-gate motifs giving INTERACTION ground truth (jointly-necessary edge pairs), the only way to score interaction recovery. Molecular (chemically meaningful functional groups): MUTAG, Mutagenicity, BBBP. Citation node classification: Cora, CiteSeer, PubMed. Large-scale (matching GNNShap scalability claims [1][17]): Reddit, ogbn-arxiv, ogbn-products. Models: GCN [12], GAT [14], GraphSAGE [15], GIN [16] at 2-3 layers (interactions present), plus a 1-layer control (interactions absent) where WEAVE MUST equal GNNShap, doubling as a collapse / vanishing-interaction check.

Metrics. Match GNNShap: fidelity+ / fidelity-, fidelity@sparsity (including 30% sparsity), fidelity@top-10 edges (expected ~unchanged pooled, since the loss is SILENT here by design). Ground-truth EDGE accuracy / AUC on synthetic motifs (sanity versus prior explainers [18][19]). NEW interaction metrics (primary contributions): (i) interaction-recovery score / AUC on planted AND/XOR motifs -- does phi_{e1,e2} capture the planted synergy, and is it placed on the TRUE jointly-necessary pair (PRIMARY NEW METRIC); (ii) surrogate reconstruction residual || f - g_additive || versus || f - g_WEAVE ||, the explicit measurement of the silent loss / interaction mass recovered; (iii) E_collapse verifier value (must be <= 1e-6 at order 1); (iv) runtime / throughput versus GNNShap at a fixed sample budget (scalability parity). Non-gameability check for (i): interaction terms placed OFF the true motif are penalized; report precision of interaction placement, not just magnitude.

Baselines (B0-B7). B0 GNNShap (additive KernelSHAP) -- the EXACT-COLLAPSE TARGET and primary comparison [1]. B1 GraphSVX [8]. B2 SubgraphX [7]. B3 GNNExplainer [18]. B4 PGExplainer [19]. B5 GNN-LRP [9] -- KEY comparison, also walk-level but not Shapley-axiomatic. B6 generic Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap FULL O(E^2) interaction [4][5] -- shown intractable / heavily approximated at scale, with WEAVE's walk-restriction as the tractable counterpart. B7 SHAP interaction values [2] (model-agnostic interaction baseline). Ablations: g_int on/off; order / depth L in {1, 2, 3}; walk-restricted T_L versus full O(E^2) support (does restriction lose real mass or just cost?); interaction-index choice Shapley-Taylor versus Faith-Shap; with/without the exact-collapse CI gate.

Falsifiable hypotheses (ranges + refutation conditions). H1 (interaction recovery): on planted AND-gate motifs, WEAVE interaction-recovery AUC in 0.80-0.95 versus additive baselines (B0/B3/B4) ~0.50 (chance); REFUTED if WEAVE AUC <= additive baseline + 0.05. H2 (silent-loss / residual): surrogate residual reduction || f - g_WEAVE || versus || f - g_additive || of 10-40% on multi-layer (2-3 layer) GNNs, and ~0% on the 1-layer control; REFUTED if the 1-layer residual changes by > 1e-6 (collapse violated) OR the multi-layer reduction < 5%. H3 (scalability parity): WEAVE runtime within 1.2x-3x of GNNShap on ogbn-products at a fixed coalition-sample budget; REFUTED if > 5x. Expected outcomes, stated as hypotheses not facts: we EXPECT WEAVE to recover walk-level interaction mass invisible to additive KernelSHAP (H1, H2), to collapse exactly to GNNShap at order 1 / 1-layer (E_collapse <= 1e-6), and to stay within a small constant factor of GNNShap runtime (H3). If H1-H3 fail under their refutation conditions, the SHEAR gap is either not present, not walk-localizable, or not recoverable at acceptable cost -- a clean negative result.

### Responsible-AI Considerations

WEAVE is methods research on public benchmarks with no deployment and no human-subjects component; the responsible-AI review returns a YELLOW (advisory) verdict with one GREEN-endorsed design property and no RED issues. Seven folds integrate as additive reporting and framing practices that do not change the algorithm. (1) Faithfulness and over-trust: WEAVE explains the MODEL, not the world; a high phi_{e1,e2} means the model's output depends on the joint presence of two edges under the chosen walk-restriction and value function, not a causal mechanism in the molecule, social tie, or transaction. We report per-interaction uncertainty (CIs / variance, since estimates are sampling-based) and state this disclaimer explicitly. (2) Dual-use: higher-fidelity attributions can guide adversarial perturbations, aid link-stealing / membership inference, or assist de-anonymization; we add an acceptable-use framing and a privacy-leakage evaluation of the explanations versus GNNShap and a no-explanation baseline, noting attribution-noising / DP-style budgets as future work. (3) Data governance: we document provenance and licenses for every dataset, prefer synthetic motif graphs for figures, and pseudonymize or redact node identifiers in any released real-graph subgraphs. (4) Fairness: we report fidelity, collapse error, and interaction quality stratified per community, class, and degree-stratum (not only macro-average), and add a fairness-backfire check on whether WEAVE concentrates attribution on sensitive-attribute-aligned edges more than GNNShap. (5) Human-in-the-loop: in high-stakes domains (drug discovery, fraud, healthcare) WEAVE outputs are advisory hypotheses requiring expert validation and logged sign-off, never automated decision rules. (6) GREEN-endorsed: the exact-collapse CI gate is a built-in safety mechanism -- a deterministic, regression-proof default (turning interactions off recovers the trusted baseline within E_collapse <= 1e-6); we keep it a hard merge requirement and release the verifier for third-party re-confirmation. (7) Reproducibility: we pin and publish seeds, sample counts, knob values (g_int, L), dataset versions, and commit hashes, and report variance for all stochastic metrics.

## Section 3: Novel Insights and Recommendations

The contribution in one place: WEAVE shows that the right way to fix an additive Shapley explainer for a structured model is not to abandon additivity but to add exactly the interaction terms the model's own computation creates, restricted to where it creates them, with a provable collapse back to the additive baseline. SHEAR names the representational-impossibility limit -- the additive surrogate [2] cannot carry the walk-level synergy f(z') - g(z') that k-layer message passing [11] computes -- and WEAVE supplies the matching field via walk-restricted Shapley-Taylor / Faith-Shap interaction terms [4][5][6] on co-walk edge tuples T_L, inheriting GNNShap's scalable estimation [1][17] and provably reducing to it at order 1.

The reusable principle generalizes beyond graphs: additive attribution cannot express a model's own interaction structure, so the remedy is to add structure-restricted interaction terms with an exact-collapse guarantee. The structure that defines the admissible interaction support is dictated by the model class -- walks for message-passing GNNs [9][11], and by analogy attention paths for transformers or interaction layers for factorization models. The exact-collapse property is what makes such an extension safe to adopt: it converts a richer but riskier explainer into a strict superset of the trusted one, the only form in which a faithfulness upgrade tends to survive into production.

Recommendations for the GNN-explainability field. First, evaluate faithfulness beyond fidelity@sparsity and fidelity@top-k: those metrics are blind to interaction mis-attribution by construction, so the community should adopt interaction-aware evaluation -- co-walk synergy recovery, AND-gate / XOR-gate detection, and surrogate reconstruction residual -- alongside ranking-based fidelity, building on principled attribution-evaluation efforts [29] and the lessons of mask- and surrogate-based explainers [18][19][10]. Second, benchmarks should ship INTERACTION ground truth (jointly-necessary edge tuples), not only motif-edge labels, so that interaction recovery is measurable at all. Third, scalable Shapley baselines [1][8] should be reported with an explicit interaction-residual number so readers can see how much joint causation a purely additive explanation leaves on the table. What WEAVE unlocks scientifically: once faithful, signed, axiom-grounded [3] interaction coefficients are computable at scale, GNN explanation becomes a hypothesis-generation instrument -- functional-group discovery in molecules [13][20][24], collusion-ring discovery in fraud graphs [25], and cooperative-motif libraries comparable across models -- moving the field from "which edges" to "which edge interactions the model actually computes," while the YELLOW-verdict responsible-AI folds keep the interpretation honest (model-attribution, not causal claim).

Evidence and verification. The central engineering guarantee is an exact-collapse identity: at interaction order 1 (equivalently g_int = 0) the admissible-tuple set T_L = { S : |S| >= 2 and S lies on a common <=L-hop walk } is empty, so the WEAVE surrogate g_WEAVE(z') = phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i + sum_{S in T_L} phi_S prod_{i in S} z'_i reduces term-by-term to phi_0 + sum_i phi_i z'_i, which is identically g_GNNShap(z'). Because the per-edge main effects are estimated under the SAME KernelSHAP weighting, attr_WEAVE,order1 == attr_GNNShap, and the residual E_collapse = || attr_WEAVE,order1 - attr_GNNShap || <= 1e-6 is enforced as a CI gate (the build fails if it is exceeded) and reported with confidence intervals. This identity is not an empirical fit but an algebraic consequence of the empty-support reduction, which is why it can be gated deterministically. The SHEAR claim it complements rests on the undisputed additivity of SHAP / KernelSHAP [2][3]: a first-order surrogate cannot represent terms outside its linear span, so the walk-level interaction content is provably absent from the additive explanation and must be supplied by the restricted interaction channel. All quantitative claims in Question 5 are stated as falsifiable hypotheses with explicit ranges and refutation conditions; none are reported as established results.
